title: NEED: copy results of a formula column to another text column
excerpt: I currently have a formula column that takes one of 3 status options and outputs a text result in the column depending if they choose option 1, 2 or 3. So let’s say the result is A, B and C. I’m trying to find some way t…

posts.author: OrangeBird (Ken Barnett)
posts.cookedHtml: I currently have a formula column that takes one of 3 status options and outputs a text result in the column depending if they choose option 1, 2 or 3. So let’s say the result is A, B and C. I’m trying to find some way to copy the result (A, B or C) from the formula column to another text column so that I can include that result into yet another automation since I can’t bring the formula column directly into the automation.

posts.author: OrangeBird (Ken Barnett)
posts.cookedHtml: note: general caster is NOT working for this solution. It is outputting the result #name? in the new column but the formula is valid and functioning in the formula column.

posts.author: becca_colesoft (Becca C)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same problem - did you ever find a solution?

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @becca_colesoft

If you’re open to trying a third-party app, you can check out Column to Column. It includes a recipe for copying data from a formula column to a standard column:

image1063×337 27 KB

If you have any question or run into issues, reach out to us!

posts.author: hellotimi (David Kalu (Formula Pro - Use formula column results in automations!))
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @OrangeBird,

Regarding your question about getting formula results to trigger automations.

We’ve just published a detailed walkthrough in the community that directly addresses this challenge. It shows you how to use your formula column results to kick off automations.

You might find it really helpful: Copy formula result to other columns and trigger automations

Hope this helps you out!
